channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: As the fire roared down a hillside toward their Altadena home, Vanessa Prata and her parents hurried to pack their car. They focused on saving irreplaceable items, like family photographs and a baby doll from Vanessa's childhood. But they didn't leave.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires ripping through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures while burning through an area larger than the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurts tosses 2 TD passes and Goedert throws 3 stiff-arms to lead Eagles past Packers 22-10
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Jalen Hurts tossed two touchdown passes in his first game since a late-season concussion, Dallas Goedert threw three stiff-arms on a rugged TD catch and the Philadelphia Eagles rode their NFL-best defense to a 22-10 wild-card playoff win over the Green Bay Packers on Sunday night
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Incarcerated firefighters join California wildfire battle efforts
item.description: The California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation has deployed 931 incarcerated firefighters and 114 support staff through its Fire Camp Program.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Jack Smith shouldn't be allowed to release report
item.description: "Why would Deranged Jack Smith be allowed to issue a “report” on a complete and total Witch Hunt against me, strictly for political purposes,” Trump said in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Biden and Netanyahu discuss the latest in the effort to reach an Israel-Hamas war ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Joe Biden spoke Sunday about efforts to reach a ceasefire and hostage release deal in the Israel-Hamas war, a sign of the intensifying push to reach a deal before Donald Trump’s inauguration next week.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wild video shows thief brazenly rip package right out of FedEx driver's arms in Massachusetts robbery
item.description: Rhode Island police arrested four suspects who led law enforcement officials on a chase that stemmed from an armed robbery of a FedEx driver in Harvard, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden speaks with relatives of Americans held by Taliban, but deal to bring them home still elusive
item.description: President Joe Biden has spoken with relatives of three Americans the U.S. government is looking to bring home from Afghanistan, but no agreement has been reached on a deal to get them back.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Southern areas hit by winter storm thaw and power slowly returns
item.description: After a freezing winter storm shut schools, cut power and cancelled or delayed flights, the South was slowly thawing Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chrisley, who gained fame on the “Chrisley Knows Best” show that featured his parents and close-knit family, was allegedly drunk at a Twin Peaks restaurant on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: (The Hill) -- Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump. “I think it’s very simple.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge that fell in strong wind closed the main highway south of Anchorage, Alaska, early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some people across Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that give respite from heat and air pollution. The tree advocates have confronted increasing drought, bad trimming and objections from neighbors who resent leaves and sap.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles burglary suspect disguised as firefighter, as looting arrests reach about 29 people: sheriff
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna said a burglary suspect disguised as a firefighter, as police arrested about 29 for looting and other criminal activity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japanese mob boss pleads guilty in New York to conspiring to traffic nuclear materials to Iran
item.description: Japanese Yakuza mob boss Takeshi Ebisawa pleaded guilty in Manhattan federal court to conspiring to traffic nuclear materials to Iran among other charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman killed in Indiana standoff drugged daughter, left her in burning apartment: officials
item.description: The woman who died following a standoff that led to an officer-involved shooting at an Indiana hotel Wednesday has been identified, the Allen County Coroner’s Office announced.
item.dc:creator: Mia Pettigrew, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: IRS delays after ID thefts 'unconscionable,' watchdog says. Here are 3 ways to protect yourself
item.description: A new tax season is nearly here, and with it an opportunity for identity thieves to take advantage of taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: A California man whose two cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu risk says he meant to keep his beloved pets healthy, but his efforts tragically backfired.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled to make further progress against wildfires that have killed at least 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Jae Hong, Holly Ramer And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tennessee man arrested after shooting at family sledding in the snow, deputies say
item.description: A Tennessee man was arrested on charges of aggravated assault after he allegedly opened fire on a family that was sledding in the snow, deputies said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texans get 2 points on blocked extra point return vs. Chargers, 1st in NFL playoff history
item.description: Houston's D'Angelo Ross returned a blocked extra point for two points in the fourth quarter of the Texans' wild-card playoff victory over the Los Angeles Chargers on Saturday — the first such play in NFL postseason history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, WGN 9

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA's fire chief is at the center of a public spat with City Hall as wildfires rage
item.description: Kristin Crowley was elevated to Los Angeles fire chief in 2022 at a time of turmoil in a department consumed by complaints of rampant hazing, harassment and discrimination among its 3,400-member ranks. As a career firefighter, she was portrayed by the then-mayor as a stabilizing force.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Santa Anita postpones weekend racing as track is staging area for fire relief efforts
item.description: A flareup of the wildfire on the west side of Los Angeles that prompted new evacuations has caused Santa Anita to cancel horse racing this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing Southern California wildfires
item.description: Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) Chief Kristin Crowley said during an interview Friday that the city failed the department amid the ongoing wildfires in Southern California that have taken at least 11 lives and burned more than 37,000 acres.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4
